_note: Starter mnemonics for Chinese vocab. Keyed by entry text. Never overwrites user-written mnemonics.
你好: 你 (nǐ) = you + 好 (hǎo) = good. Literally 'you good' — a friendly check-in. Think: 'You good?' as a casual hello.
谢谢: Doubled for emphasis. 谢 looks like a character bowing twice in gratitude. Sounds like 'shyeh-shyeh' — picture someone shyly bowing twice.
苹果: 苹 (píng) sounds like 'ping' — imagine a ping-pong ball bouncing off an apple 🍎.
水: The character shows three drops of water flowing down — like a river seen from above. 水 (shuǐ) ≈ 'shway', like water swaying.
书: 书 (shū) sounds like 'shoe' — imagine a book shaped like a shoe 👟📖.
猫: 猫 (māo) sounds like a cat's meow — 'mao!' The left side 犭 is the animal radical. Cats say their own name in Chinese.
狗: 狗 (gǒu) has the dog radical 犭 on the left. Sounds like 'go' — a dog that always goes fetch.
吃: 吃 (chī) has the mouth radical 口 on the left — mouths eat. Sounds like 'chee', like chewing.
喝: 喝 (hē) also has the mouth radical 口 — mouths drink too. 'hē' sounds like a sigh of relief after a good drink: 'ahh'.
大: 大 (dà) is a person 人 with arms stretched wide — showing how BIG something is. 'dà' like 'dah, it's huge!'
小: 小 (xiǎo) shows a small dot flanked by two tiny strokes — three small things. Like a miniature version of big.
好: 好 (hǎo) = 女 (woman) + 子 (child) = good. A mother with her child — the classic image of goodness and happiness.
人: 人 (rén) literally looks like a person standing with legs apart — a stick figure. Simple and iconic.
朋友: 朋 (péng) = two moons side by side — friends are like two moons shining together. 友 (yǒu) shows a hand being offered in friendship.
学生: 学 (xué) = to study/learn; 生 (shēng) = life/birth. A student is someone whose life is learning. xuéshēng.
老师: 老 (lǎo) = old/elder; 师 (shī) = master/expert. A teacher is a wise elder. lǎoshī — 'lao' like 'low' respect bowed to.
工作: 工 (gōng) = work/labor — looks like an I-beam used in construction. 作 (zuò) = to do/make. Together: doing labor.
学习: 学 (xué) = learn; 习 (xí) = practice/review. Learning is studying AND practicing. xuéxí — study then repeat.
喜欢: 喜 (xǐ) = joy; 欢 (huān) = happy/welcome. Double happiness — to like something is to feel joy and welcome it.
漂亮: 漂 (piào) has the water radical 氵 — like something floating beautifully on water. 亮 (liàng) = bright. Beautiful = brightly floating.
高兴: 高 (gāo) = tall/high; 兴 (xìng) = excitement/interest. Happy = your spirits are HIGH and excited. gāoxìng!
快: 快 (kuài) has the heart radical 忄— fast comes from the heart. Also sounds like 'quick'. Your heart beats FAST.
慢: 慢 (màn) also has the heart radical 忄— slow comes from the heart too, but lazily. Sounds like 'man… take it slow'.
买: 买 (mǎi) = to buy. Sounds like 'my' — 'that's MY apple, I bought it!' The top shows a net to catch bargains.
钱: 钱 (qián) has the metal/gold radical 钅on the left — money is made of metal. Sounds like 'chyen' — jingle of coins.
医生: 医 (yī) = medicine/heal; 生 (shēng) = life. A doctor heals lives. yīshēng — the life-giver.
经济: 经 (jīng) = pass through/manage; 济 (jì) = benefit/help. Economy = managing resources to benefit people.
尽管: 尽 (jǐn) = exhaust/use up; 管 (guǎn) = manage/control. 'Although' — even after exhausting all control, still...
人: 人 (rén) looks like a person walking — two legs striding forward. The simplest picture of a human being.
